creator: kh@mogami.com
text:
In ja_JP.eucJP locale environment (ports/x11/)xterm and (ports/japanese/)kterm 
crushes with segmentation fault.

How-To-Repeat:

With 11.2-RELEASE(amd64|i386) and installed port/x11/xorg only,

1) add bellow line to /root/.profile (which is default)
  export LC_ALL="ja_JP.eucJP"
2) login as root then type
  startx (will produces 3 xterm windows)
3) In one of xterm window press key like
  Alt-A (or Alt-B, Alt-C, .. Alt-1, Alt-4, ..)

xterm will crush with segmentation fault.

This problem also occures in ports/japanese/kterm,
and it makes impossible input or paste japanese kanji character.
For the case of kterm, in charproc.c bcnt = read() (opened with FIONBIO)
returns 0 then called Panic().

This problem does not occur in the 10.4-RELEASE installing the *same*
ports.tar.gz, so it suggests kernel bug.
